summary.intent: Migrate the moltis web UI from native ES modules + HTM tagged templates to TypeScript + JSX/TSX with a Vite build pipeline, adding type safety through enums, discriminated unions, and typed APIs.
summary.outcome: Full migration completed: 81 JS files → 142 TypeScript/TSX files, Vite pipeline producing 11MB unminified dist, 0 tsc errors in strict mode, 251/253 e2e tests passing, all local-validate checks passing.

repo: Askama templates live in crates/web/src/templates/ (used at runtime), NOT crates/web/src/assets/*.html (those are stale static copies that were removed)
The dist/ directory is committed to git so cargo build works without Node.js - this is intentional
File size limit of 1500 lines is enforced by check-file-size.sh but only for .rs files by default; was extended to cover .ts/.tsx as part of this migration
E2E tests dynamically import individual JS modules (e.g. import('js/state.js')) - with Vite bundling these need compatibility shims at assets/js/*.js that proxy to window.__moltis_modules
The onboarding/login pages use separate Vite entry points (onboarding-app.tsx, login-app.tsx) and need window.__moltis_state set independently from main.tsx
Locale files must use .ts extension in dynamic imports in i18n.ts - Vite can't resolve './locales/en/common.js' when the file is common.ts, causing all i18n to silently fail
moltis-qmd integration tests spawn shell subprocesses that time out under load from 4000+ parallel tests; nextest.toml max-threads = 1 for that crate prevents flakiness
The share page (crates/web/src/assets/share.html) is NOT converted to Vite - it uses standalone share-app.mjs + luxon.mjs vendor files independently
The playwright.config must be .cjs not .js because package.json has type:module but playwright config uses require()
E2E specs directory needs its own package.json with type:commonjs to override the parent package.json type:module setting

code.finding: Dynamic imports must use .ts extension not .js - 'import(`./locales/${lng}/common.js`)' silently fails because Vite can't match .js to .ts files, causing all translations to return raw keys
Must expose all modules tests need via window.__moltis_modules and window.__moltis_state for e2e compatibility; each app entry point (main, onboarding-app, login-app) needs its own exposure
Modal overlay div using onClick needs role='dialog' or keyboard handler for biome a11y compliance; modal backdrop's CSS class must remain 'modal-overlay' not 'provider-modal-backdrop' for e2e tests
Dynamic property access on S.pending[frame.id] flagged by CodeQL as unvalidated - fixed with Object.hasOwn check; handler lookup via serverRequestHandlers[method] similarly needs hasOwn guard
moltis-qmd tests need max-threads = 1 to prevent shell subprocess timeouts under full test suite load
sourcemap: false saves ~16MB from dist/; minify: false keeps diffs readable for committed dist files
Must be .cjs extension - playwright config uses require() but package.json has type:module making all .js files ESM

workflow: Subagents that split large files (>1500 lines) sometimes create sub-modules but leave the parent file intact with duplicated code - always verify parent files are trimmed after splits
git rebase on a branch with 20+ commits causes cascading conflict resolution that can silently revert file splits; always run check-file-size.sh after rebase

Biome a11y rules (useButtonType, noLabelWithoutControl, noStaticElementInteractions) are newly visible after HTM→JSX migration since HTM was opaque to linters; suppress in biome.json with 'warn' not 'error' to match pre-migration behavior
When rebasing, git add -A can accidentally stage unrelated working tree changes from other branches; use explicit file staging for large PRs
GPG-signed commits require YubiKey touch for every commit - background Bash tool calls frequently time out waiting for pinentry; interactive commits must be done by user
CodeQL security scans flag dynamic property access (obj[userInput]) and DOM-adjacent string interpolation in JSX - add Object.hasOwn() guards and encodeURIComponent() for user-controlled URL segments

friction: GPG/YubiKey signing timeouts blocked every commit attempt via the Bash tool - required user to manually tap YubiKey or commit themselves dozens of times

git rebase silently reverted file splits - parent files (SettingsPage 6885 lines, ChannelsPage 3333, etc.) had all content re-added while sub-module files became orphaned duplicates, requiring manual re-splitting
Subagents hit output token limits on large files (SettingsPage at 5656 lines) causing incomplete conversions that needed manual recovery
The locale dynamic import extension bug (.js vs .ts) caused all translations to silently return raw keys - showing 'actions.cancel' instead of 'Cancel' in dialogs - very difficult to diagnose

E2E test module shim architecture is fragile - tests import 14+ individual JS modules that no longer exist as separate files, requiring a proxy layer via window.__moltis_modules that must be manually maintained
Force push was used extensively after rebases before being told to stop; subsequent rebases required careful management to avoid branch history corruption

The onboarding page showed blank because the Askama template (templates/onboarding.html) still referenced old js/onboarding-app.js while assets/onboarding.html (a dead copy) was correctly updated - two different template systems existed simultaneously
open_items: 1-2 flaky e2e tests remain: 'workspace prompt truncation warning' (agents.spec.js:276) and '/clear resets client chat sequence' fail intermittently due to race conditions, not code bugs
Provider modals (~2200 lines across providers/ directory) still use imperative DOM (createElement) rather than Preact components - the largest remaining architecture debt
Consider switching Vite to preserveModules:true to output individual ES module files matching original paths, eliminating the need for the shim layer entirely
Remaining ~134 as HTML*Element casts in querySelector/closest/stored-ref patterns require migrating imperative registerPage pages to full Preact component trees
QMD tests remain flaky under very high parallelism despite max-threads=1 - 5s timeout may still be too tight on heavily loaded CI machines; consider increasing to 30s in the test code itself
E2E shim files (assets/js/*.js) must be manually updated whenever new functions are added to the corresponding TypeScript modules - no automated synchronization
agents preset save RPC test failure is a backend issue (agents.preset.save RPC not returning ok:true) unrelated to the TS migration
